description_long: This is a medium-sized deciduous tree with a slender, dark brown trunk and a visible root flare at the base. The canopy is composed of dense clusters of orange and brown broad leaves, suggesting an autumnal season. The branches are irregularly distributed, with some extending horizontally and others arching upward, giving the tree an asymmetrical silhouette. The foliage is thickest at the top and on one side, while the lower branches are more sparse. The tree's roots are partially exposed above the ground, adding to its naturalistic appearance. This type of tree is commonly found in urban parks, streetscapes, or residential neighborhoods, providing shade and seasonal color.
description: A medium-sized deciduous tree with a dark trunk, exposed roots, and dense orange-brown autumn foliage in an irregular canopy.
description_view_0: The tree is seen with its canopy leaning to the left, showing a prominent cluster of leaves and visible roots at the base.
description_view_1: This view highlights the tree's central trunk and the dense, rounded foliage, with the canopy appearing more compact.
description_view_2: The tree's canopy appears fuller and more symmetrical, with the trunk slightly curved and roots visible at the base.
description_view_3: The canopy leans to the right, revealing more of the trunk and the layered structure of the leaves, with roots still visible.
